name: Clam Chowder, Maine Style
recipeInstructions:
1. Dice the onions and salt pork.
2. Saute slowly in an iron frying pan until the onions are translucent and the salt pork is lightly browned.
3. Empty the contents of the frying pan into a chowder pot.
4. Add 1 quart (or more) of water to the pot, stirring to combine.
5. Bring the mixture to a boil, then add the diced potatoes and return to a boil.
6. Use a meat grinder to process the clams into small pieces.
7. Add the clam pieces to the pot and simmer until the vegetables are tender and the clams have cooked through.
8. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
9. Stir in the evaporated milk just before serving.
10. Turn off the heat and let the chowder set for a few minutes.
11. Reheat gently just before serving.
12. Serve with crackers. (Note: I made this version with canned chopped clams, which worked well!)
